message:
br-s83dv: fuzz_pathname frees realpath/canonicalize_file_name returns

LSan was reporting exit-status-77 on short runs: harness called
realpath(path, NULL) and canonicalize_file_name(path), both returning
libc::malloc'd buffers, and deliberately leaked them with a comment
claiming libfuzzer resets state between runs. It does not — LSan
catches the leak and crashes the campaign. This masked any real
library-side leaks.

Fix: pair with libc::free. Our realpath at stdlib_abi.rs:4050 uses
libc::malloc, so libc::free is the correct deallocator.

Promoted the reproducer to the corpus as a regression entry.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -394,7 +394,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-3hud","title":"bd-yos subtask: Join/detach lifecycle state machine and error semantics","description":"Background:
- join/detach lifecycle correctness is essential to avoid resource leaks and undefined ownership states.

Goal:
- Implement deterministic thread lifecycle state machine for join/detach/exit-value propagation.

Deliverables:
1) Thread handle lifecycle states.
2) Join semantics with exit status delivery.
3) Detach semantics and resource reclamation.
4) Error handling (double-join, join-detached, invalid handle).

Acceptance Criteria:
- Lifecycle operations are race-safe and deterministic under test.
- Error codes and edge behavior are explicit and verified.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests for lifecycle transitions and misuse cases.
- Structured logs: trace_id, handle_id, op, old_state, new_state, return_value_ref, errno.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:00:52.033022201Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T09:24:05.221352645Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T09:24:05.221285439Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","implementation","pthread","testing","threading"],"comments":[{"id":997,"issue_id":"bd-3hud","author":"CrimsonForge","text":"COMPLETED: Join/detach lifecycle state machine and error semantics (bd-3hud).

Changes from bd-mjon baseline:

1. CAS-protected state transitions:
   - Trampoline: STARTING -> RUNNING via compare_exchange (prevents overwriting DETACHED)
   - Trampoline: RUNNING -> FINISHED via compare_exchange (detects DETACHED for self-cleanup)
   - join_thread: FINISHED -> JOINED via CAS (only one joiner wins)
   - detach_thread: RUNNING/STARTING -> DETACHED via CAS (only one detacher wins)
   - detach_thread: FINISHED -> DETACHED via CAS (races safely with concurrent joiner)

2. Self-join detection (EDEADLK):
   - join_thread checks if calling thread's TID matches target's TID

3. Detached thread self-cleanup:
   - Trampoline detects DETACHED state via CAS failure
   - Calls set_tid_address(NULL) to prevent kernel CLONE_CHILD_CLEARTID write to freed memory
   - Frees heap-allocated ThreadHandle
   - Stack reclaimed by OS on process exit (munmap deferred to bd-rth1 for signal safety)

4. Error semantics:
   - NULL handle: EINVAL (22) for both join and detach
   - Self-join: EDEADLK (35)
   - Join already-joined: EINVAL
   - Join detached: EINVAL
   - Detach already-joined: EINVAL
   - Detach already-detached: EINVAL

5. New syscall wrapper:
   - sys_set_tid_address(tidptr) in syscall/mod.rs

Files modified:
- crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pthread/thread.rs: CAS state machine, self-cleanup, error codes
- crates/frankenlibc-core/src/syscall/mod.rs: Added sys_set_tid_address

Quality gates: all PASS
Tests: 11/11 PASS (5 new for bd-3hud)","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:23:59Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3jh","title":"Integration: C compile/link/run fixture suite under LD_PRELOAD","description":"Critique mapping: #1.

Deliverables:
- A minimal, versioned C fixture suite under tests/integration/ that:
  - links against glibc as usual
  - is executed under LD_PRELOAD=.../libc.so
  - validates behavior (stdout + errno + safety outcomes)

Acceptance:
- Fixtures cover: malloc/free/realloc, memcpy/memmove/strlen, read/write/open/close, pthread_mutex_*.
- Results are stable and checked by harness.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","notes":"2026-02-11 closure (CrimsonCove): c_fixture_suite_test (7 pass) + gate script. All fixture sources exist, acceptance symbols covered, covered modules valid, fixtures have required fields.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCove","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:37:26.849037323Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:54:16.707976Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T16:54:16.707976Z","close_reason":"C compile/link/run fixture suite operational. 7 harness tests pass. check_c_fixture_suite.sh validates fixture sources, acceptance symbols, covered modules.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","critique","e2e"]}
 {"id":"bd-3jk4","title":"process: execute process_ops.json fixture family through harness matrix","description":"Concrete gap: crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/process_ops_conformance_test.rs currently validates process fixture metadata and coverage counts only. Add isolated harness subprocess execution for tests/conformance/fixtures/process_ops.json, assert expected_output and host parity in strict/hardened modes, and fix any matrix-path regressions the new coverage exposes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T04:52:12.955090641Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T15:36:23.152748450Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T15:36:23.150567471Z","close_reason":"Executed process_ops fixture coverage through the isolated harness conformance matrix path and verified via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-3jpoz","title":"exit/quick_exit hold Mutex across user callbacks - self-deadlock on re-entrant on_exit/at_quick_exit","description":"stdlib_abi.rs::exit (lines 773-778) holds ON_EXIT_HANDLERS.lock() while invoking user callbacks. stdlib_abi.rs::quick_exit (lines 2355-2360) holds QUICK_EXIT_HANDLERS.lock() across user callback invocation. Any handler that transitively calls on_exit or at_quick_exit self-deadlocks on the non-recursive std::sync::Mutex. Class 1. Fix: swap-extract the Vec under the lock, drop guard, iterate extracted vec (pattern copied from frankenlibc_core::stdlib::run_atexit_handlers which already does this correctly with an outer loop for newly-registered handlers). Sibling fix to bd-sq7ae which fixed the same anti-pattern in pthread_abi.rs::run_atfork_*.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T17:40:30.975453666Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:42:00.000000000Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"closed_at":"2026-04-23T17:42:00.000000000Z","close_reason":"Fixed: swap-extract on_exit/at_quick_exit handlers instead of holding lock across callbacks. Landed in 7fca17e9."}
+{"id":"bd-3jpoz","title":"exit/quick_exit hold Mutex across user callbacks - self-deadlock on re-entrant on_exit/at_quick_exit","description":"stdlib_abi.rs::exit (lines 773-778) holds ON_EXIT_HANDLERS.lock() while invoking user callbacks. stdlib_abi.rs::quick_exit (lines 2355-2360) holds QUICK_EXIT_HANDLERS.lock() across user callback invocation. Any handler that transitively calls on_exit or at_quick_exit self-deadlocks on the non-recursive std::sync::Mutex. Class 1. Fix: swap-extract the Vec under the lock, drop guard, iterate extracted vec (pattern copied from frankenlibc_core::stdlib::run_atexit_handlers which already does this correctly with an outer loop for newly-registered handlers). Sibling fix to bd-sq7ae which fixed the same anti-pattern in pthread_abi.rs::run_atfork_*.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T17:40:30.975453666Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:42:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T17:42:00Z","close_reason":"Fixed: swap-extract on_exit/at_quick_exit handlers instead of holding lock across callbacks. Landed in 7fca17e9.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3jzb","title":"BRAVO RW Locks for read-heavy paths (section 14.7)","description":"Implement BRAVO (Biased Reader And Visible Observer) reader-writer locks for read-heavy paths in FrankenLibC. BRAVO is a RW lock optimized for the common case of read-dominant workloads. Readers use a visible-reader protocol (write their TID to a shared slot on read entry, clear on exit). Writers check all slots for active readers. Key innovation: biased locking — the fast path for readers is a single atomic write (no CAS, no fence on x86-TSO). Writers pay the cost of scanning reader slots. Application to FrankenLibC: (1) Allocation metadata — the per-arena metadata (free list head, allocation count, generation counters) is read on every allocation for validation but written infrequently (only on actual alloc/free state changes). BRAVO eliminates reader contention. (2) TSM certificate cache — evaluated certificates are cached. Cache reads vastly outnumber writes (cache invalidation is rare). (3) Thread registry — the list of active threads is read by garbage collection and RCU grace period detection but modified only on thread creation/destruction. Performance target: reader fast path <5ns (single atomic store), writer path <100ns (slot scan + fence). Comparison: implement alongside RCU (bd-3aof) and Left-Right (bd-cs9w) — benchmark all three for FrankenLibC's specific read/write ratios to determine which is optimal for each use case.

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","design":"**Alien CS Reference:** Section 14.7 of graveyard. BRAVO from Dice, Kogan, & Herlihy (2019 USENIX ATC). Visible-reader protocol for biased RW locks optimized for read-dominant workloads.

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- BravoRwLock<T>: wraps T with visible-reader protocol.
- Reader fast path: atomic store of TID to slot in reader table. Single store on x86-TSO (no fence needed).
- Reader table: #[repr(align(64))] array of AtomicU64 slots, sized to MAX_THREADS.
- Writer: acquire inner lock (Mutex), scan reader table for active readers, wait for all to clear.
- Slot assignment: hash(thread_id) mod TABLE_SIZE for O(1) slot lookup. Collision handling: linear probe.
- read() -> BravoReadGuard: write TID to slot, read data, clear slot on drop.
- write() -> BravoWriteGuard: acquire mutex, scan table, return mutable reference.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
1. BravoRwLock<T> compiles and passes miri for basic read/write cycle.
2. Reader fast path <5ns at p99 (single atomic store on x86-TSO).
3. Writer path <100ns at p99 including slot scan under 16-reader load.
4. 32-reader + 1-writer stress test: 1M operations, TSan clean.
5. Compare with RCU and Left-Right: benchmark all three on FrankenLibC metadata workload.
6. No reader starvation: readers always make progress regardless of writer activity.
7. Slot collision rate <5% at 64 threads (hash quality test).

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"**Logging Requirements:**
- tracing::trace!(target: bravo, operation=read, slot, reader_count) per read (disabled in release).
- tracing::debug!(target: bravo, operation=write, scan_time_ns, active_readers_found) per write.
- Benchmark comparison: bravo_vs_rcu_vs_leftright.json with throughput and latency per read/write ratio.

### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Codex","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:29:46.141109287Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T23:04:14.456054507Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T23:04:14.455840361Z","close_reason":"Delivered: code + artifacts present.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3jzb","depends_on_id":"bd-3aof","type":"related","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3kh","title":"Kernel: Proof-carrying policy tables (integrate into decide/repair selection)","description":"Wire policy table into runtime decision.

Rules:
- Strict mode: table should not change externally visible semantics; only controls validation depth.
- Hardened mode: table may choose Repair vs Deny vs FullValidate, but must respect barrier constraints.

Outputs:
- Snapshot exports policy hash and selected action distribution.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:33:33.862452216Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T02:04:10.442423867Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T01:50:19.509740823Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3kh","depends_on_id":"bd-20s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3kh","depends_on_id":"bd-2vf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":998,"issue_id":"bd-3kh","author":"FuchsiaHollow","text":"Integrated policy table into decide() with policy_escalation pattern. PolicyTableLookup struct provides O(1) lookup from owned bytes. Conservative merge: table can only escalate profile, never de-escalate. Action cascade order invariant preserved by extracting Deny/Repair/FullValidate into policy_escalation variable (Allow defers to normal cascade). Snapshot exports policy_hash_prefix and action distribution. All 788 membrane tests pass, full workspace clean.","created_at":"2026-02-11T01:49:58Z"},{"id":999,"issue_id":"bd-3kh","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CobaltCompass: Fixed strict-mode Deny bug - PCPT Deny/Repair clamp to FullValidate in strict mode. Added 6 integration tests. All 794 membrane tests pass. Golden snapshot and perf baseline updated.","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:04:10Z"}]}

Actual compilation via gcc has a -plugin linker argument issue (ld gets wrong args). Tracked separately.","created_at":"2026-03-25T05:12:29Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-s39o.3","title":"Test and fix network diagnostic tools under LD_PRELOAD (wget, dig, nslookup)","description":"Test wget, dig, nslookup, host under LD_PRELOAD.
These exercise: DNS resolution (getaddrinfo beyond /etc/hosts), socket connect, SSL/TLS, HTTP parsing.
Our getaddrinfo only supports numeric hosts and /etc/hosts lookups. For DNS resolution, we'd need to either:
1. Delegate to host libc's getaddrinfo for non-numeric hosts
2. Implement a DNS resolver stub
Option 1 is more practical for now.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:54.277849234Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T05:13:31.400999331Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T05:13:30.152849132Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-s39o.3","depends_on_id":"bd-cmiv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:16.988805266Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-s39o.3","depends_on_id":"bd-s39o","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:54.277849234Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1338,"issue_id":"bd-s39o.3","author":"ubuntu","text":"wget resolves DNS, connects, but GnuTLS has thread locking error (separate SSL issue). curl works perfectly including HTTPS. dig/nslookup are statically linked (can't preload). host works.","created_at":"2026-03-25T05:13:31Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-s83dv","title":"fuzz_pathname harness: leaks realpath(NULL) return instead of freeing","description":"crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_pathname.rs:153-158 calls realpath(cs.as_ptr(), NULL), gets a malloc'd buffer, and deliberately leaks it (comment: 'leak in the fuzz target is acceptable'). LeakSanitizer flags this and exits with 77 as soon as a path bytes combination makes realpath succeed (resolving to something short, e.g. 2 bytes). Effect: short fuzz runs fail on an infrastructural leak, masking any real library-side leak. Fix: unsafe { libc::free(ret as *mut c_void) } after archetype completes. Our realpath at stdlib_abi.rs:4050 uses libc::malloc so libc::free is the right pair. Reproducer: crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/artifacts/fuzz_pathname/leak-f413061db6eee949e03cba98bbb1b4526ec3525c","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:01:39.200746584Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:01:39.200746584Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-sbt21","title":"sprintf executor returns 'unsupported printf arg value' for float %e","description":"execute_sprintf_case in frankenlibc_conformance cannot dispatch certain printf conversions (observed: sprintf_e_basic with 12345.0). Surfaced while wiring bd-12hh printf_conformance harness matrix. Those specific fixture cases are skipped in the harness-matrix tests until the executor gains broader conversion support.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T20:00:37.882801746Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T05:46:00.070271168Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T05:46:00.068275622Z","close_reason":"parse_printf_args now always routes JSON float literals to PrintfArg::Double; added PrintfArg::NullPtr + 3/4/5-arg dispatch arms for star-width, multiple-conversion, mixed-type, and consecutive-int fixtures. Remaining executor gaps tracked in bd-0u5fm/bd-luc3d/bd-vgrav/bd-qij6l via KNOWN_IMPL_GAPS. Committed a4789ab.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-sh2ms","title":"syscall: pidfd_getfd self pidfd with invalid flags should return EINVAL exactly","description":"Host glibc returns EINVAL for pidfd_getfd(self_pidfd, 1, 1). Add focused ABI regression coverage for the invalid-flags contract on a valid pidfd.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:52:52.477053379Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-22T04:54:24.500827639Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T04:54:24.498299060Z","close_reason":"pidfd_getfd(self, 0, 1) → EINVAL (non-zero flags reserved) pinned.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
